title: Rule-Obj-FieldValue
description: JSON schema for creating or updating a Pega field value rule (Rule-Obj-FieldValue). Field values store localizable text strings — labels, messages, captions, prompts, button text, notification messages, and error classifications — associated with a property on a class. Only authored and identity fields are included; system-managed and versioning fields are omitted.

pyClassName.description: The applies-to class for this field value (e.g., '@baseclass', 'MyOrg-MyApp-Work-MyCase', 'Pega-API'). Use '@baseclass' for values available across all classes.

pyFieldName.description: The property name this field value belongs to. Common values: 'pyMessageLabel' (API/UI messages), 'pyCaption' (labels/captions), 'pyButtonLabel' (button text), 'pyNotificationMessage' (flow notifications), 'pyGenerativeAIPrompt' (user prompt for GenAI connectors -- referenced by pyGenAIDef.pyUserPrompt), 'pyGenerativeAISystemPrompt' (system prompt for GenAI connectors -- referenced by pyGenAIDef.pySystemPrompt), 'pyErrorClassification' (error categories), 'pyActionPrompt' (helper/tooltip text), 'pyMessage' (general-purpose messages), 'pzErrorMessage' (internal platform error messages), 'pxDecisioningClass' (strategy result class mapping for decisioning). Together with pyFieldValue, forms the unique identity key.

pyFieldValue.description: The unique value identifier within the pyFieldName (e.g., 'InvalidStepID', 'pzAllowedStartingFields', 'NotifyComplianceOfficer_0'). Together with pyFieldName, forms the unique identity key.

pyLabel.description: Human-readable display label for this field value.

pyLocalizedValue.description: The actual text content. Supports two substitution mechanisms: (1) numbered placeholders like {1}, {2} resolved via pyFieldValueParams entries, and (2) inline Pega property reference expressions like {.pyPropertyName} or {.pyPageList(N).pyField} resolved directly from the clipboard at runtime. This is the primary authored content of the rule.

pyRuleName.description: Internal rule name — auto-derived as 'pyFieldName pyFieldValue' (e.g., 'pyMessageLabel InvalidStepID').

pyDescription.description: Developer-facing description of this field value's purpose.

pyUsage.description: Usage notes — where and how this field value is referenced.

pyMemo.description: Developer change memo saved with the rule.

pyRuleSet.description: Rule set the field value is saved to.

pyDisplayMode.description: Display mode for the rule form. 'basic' for new rules, 'clone' for copied rules.

pyValueChanged.description: Tracks whether the localized value has been modified from its original.

pyRuleStarts.description: Effective start date. Default is epoch (no restriction).

pyRuleEnds.description: Effective end date. Default is epoch (no restriction).

pySortDateCircumWithinRSMajor.description: Internal sort flag for circumstance resolution within a ruleset major version.

pyCircumstanceProp.description: Property used for circumstancing. Empty for no circumstancing. Advanced — only needed for circumstanced rule variants.

pyBaseRule.description: Whether this is a platform-defined base rule.

pyEditable.description: Whether the field value is editable at runtime.

pyMethodStatus.description: Method status. Usually empty for application rules, 'Internal' for platform-internal rules.

pyFieldValueParams.description: Message parameters for parameterized text. Each entry maps a placeholder ({1}, {2}, etc.) in pyLocalizedValue to a property reference or default value. Order matches placeholder numbering.

MessageParameter.description: A message parameter entry (Embed-Message-Parameter). Maps a {N} placeholder in pyLocalizedValue to a property reference.

pyValue.description: Parameter key/identifier that maps to the {N} placeholder by position. Can be a numeric string matching the placeholder number (e.g., '1' for {1}), or a descriptive key name (e.g., 'channelType'). This is the name the calling code uses to reference this parameter.

pyDefault.description: Default value expression that provides the substitution text at runtime. Typically a property reference (e.g., 'Application.pyLabel', '.pyLabel') resolved from the clipboard. Can also be a literal string or empty.
